product_overview: Zoc Studio Agent is a desktop AI workspace with an integrated agent chat panel, session management, and settings for configuring providers. Users can select models, send prompts to an agent, and view streamed responses and run activity in a unified shell.
core_goals: Configure an AI provider key and discover available models
Choose an active model for agent interactions
Send prompts to the agent and view streamed responses
Create, switch, rename, and delete chat sessions

features.name: Settings provider setup for Groq
features.description: Users can add a Groq API key in Settings, discover available models, and make Groq the active provider for agent use.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open Settings -> Switch to the Providers section -> Locate the Groq provider card -> Expand or edit the Groq provider details -> Paste the Groq API key -> Discover models -> See a populated Groq model list -> Select Groq as the active provider

Navigate to / -> Open Settings -> Switch to the Providers section -> Locate the Groq provider card -> Expand or edit the Groq provider details -> Paste an invalid Groq API key -> Discover models -> See no usable model list update

features.name: Agent model picker
features.description: Users can choose which model the agent panel uses, including a Groq-hosted model or a local llama.cpp model entry.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open the model picker in the Agent panel -> Select a Groq model -> See the active model label update
Navigate to / -> Open the model picker in the Agent panel -> Select a local llama.cpp model -> See the model badge update to a loading, loaded, or error state

features.name: Agent chat with streamed responses
features.description: Users can send prompts to the agent and watch the response stream into the conversation timeline, including tool call details and run status updates when applicable.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type a coding-related prompt into the agent composer -> Send the prompt -> See the user message appear -> See an assistant response stream into the timeline -> See the run status complete
Navigate to / -> Type a prompt -> Switch the composer to Ask mode -> Send the prompt -> See a streamed assistant answer without a review step
Navigate to / -> Type a prompt -> Start a long-running agent response -> Stop the run -> See streaming halt

features.name: Agent run review and change application
features.description: Users can review agent-generated file changes, inspect per-file diffs, and either apply or discard the run after it finishes.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Send a code change request in Agent mode -> Watch the agent work through its run -> See a review card with aggregated diffs appear -> Expand a file diff -> Apply the changes -> See the run marked applied

Navigate to / -> Send a code change request in Agent mode -> Watch the agent work through its run -> See a review card with aggregated diffs appear -> Discard the changes -> See the run marked discarded

features.name: Sessions panel
features.description: Users can manage chat sessions by creating new sessions, switching between them, renaming them, and deleting them.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Open the Sessions panel -> Create a new session -> See the new session appear and become active
Navigate to / -> Open the Sessions panel -> Select an existing session -> See the conversation switch to that session
Navigate to / -> Open the Sessions panel -> Open a session context menu -> Rename the session -> See the updated session name
Navigate to / -> Open the Sessions panel -> Open a session context menu -> Delete the session -> See the session removed from the list

features.name: First-run onboarding workspace selection
features.description: On first launch, users can choose a workspace folder to begin using the desktop app.
user_flows: Navigate to / on first launch -> Choose a workspace folder -> Finish onboarding -> See the main app shell load

tech_stack: FastAPI agent sidecar (Python backend, OpenAI-compatible bridge for Groq / OpenAI / Gemini / xAI / Anthropic)

routes.description: Single-page Zoc Studio desktop shell with activity bar, side panel, agent panel, bottom dock, and Settings overlay

features.name: Settings -> Providers -> Groq BYOK key flow
features.description:
In Settings, the Providers section lists built-in providers including Groq (baseUrl https://api.groq.com/openai/v1).
The tester must:
  1) Open Settings (gear icon in the activity bar).
  2) Switch to the "Providers" tab/section.
  3) Find the Groq card.
  4) Click "Edit" or expand the Groq card to reveal the API key field.
  5) Paste the Groq API key into the key field.
  6) Click the "Save" or "Discover models" button.
  7) Confirm a non-empty list of Groq models appears (e.g. llama-3.3-70b-versatile, llama-3.1-8b-instant).
  8) Mark Groq as the active provider if a Set-Active / Select control is shown.
Test API key (test-only, will be rotated after run): REDACTED_GROQ_KEY_ROTATE_ME
user_interactions: Click the Settings icon in the activity bar
Click the "Providers" tab
Locate the Groq provider card
Click "Edit" on the Groq card
Paste the Groq API key (REDACTED_GROQ_KEY_ROTATE_ME) into the API key input
Click "Discover models" / "Save"
Verify the model list updates with real Groq models

features.name: Model picker / switcher in Agent panel
features.description:
The Agent panel header has a Model picker dropdown. After saving the Groq key in Settings,
open the picker, pick a Groq model (e.g. llama-3.3-70b-versatile), and confirm the active
model label updates. Also verify "local model" path: pick the llama.cpp / Local entry and
confirm the badge shows loading/loaded/error appropriately.
user_interactions: Click the model picker dropdown button at the top of the Agent panel
Select a Groq model (e.g. "Llama 3.3 70B Versatile")
Confirm the picker collapses and the active model badge shows the chosen model
Re-open the picker, select a Local (llama.cpp) model
Confirm a loading/loaded/error badge is rendered

features.name: Agent chat panel - send prompt, observe Claude-Code / Cursor-style streamed output
features.description:
Send a prompt in the Agent panel composer. With a real provider (Groq) configured the
assistant message should stream tokens into the timeline. The UI should render tool call
cards (ToolCallCard.tsx) and diff cards (DiffCard.tsx) where applicable, similar to the
Cursor / Claude-Code experience. If the sidecar is unreachable the UI falls back to a
mock reply -- the test should treat the literal text "Mock response" or "agent sidecar not
reachable" as a failure signal for the live-streaming assertion.
user_interactions: Type a coding-related prompt into the composer text area (e.g. "Explain the agent run flow")
Press Send (or Enter)
Confirm the user bubble appears in the timeline
Confirm an assistant bubble appears and tokens stream into it character by character
Confirm the text does NOT contain "Mock response" / "agent sidecar not reachable"
If a tool-call card appears, click the expand chevron and confirm tool input/output renders
Confirm the run lifecycle badge moves from "running" to "completed" / "awaiting review"
Click the Stop button on a long-running run and confirm the streaming halts

features.name: Every button in the main shell
features.description:
Walk through every button / icon in the activity bar, top bar, agent header, composer,
bottom dock, and Settings page. For each button: click it, confirm the expected panel /
dialog / state change is visible, and no console / UI error appears.
user_interactions: Click each activity-bar icon and confirm the side panel content switches
Click the top-bar buttons (settings gear, command-palette trigger, etc.)
Click the agent header buttons (model picker, agent menu, new session, clear, stop)
Click composer buttons (attach, slash trigger, send, stop)
Click bottom-dock tabs (terminal, problems, output, etc.)
Click every action in the Settings sections (save, reset, discover, edit, delete, add)

features.name: Sessions panel
features.description: Create / list / switch / rename / delete chat sessions.
user_interactions: Click Sessions icon in the activity bar
Click New Session
Click an existing session row to switch
Use the row context menu to rename and to delete

features.name: Onboarding wizard
user_interactions: On first launch, choose a workspace folder and click Finish

known_limitations.issue: Frontend is a Tauri desktop shell -- in a plain browser, some bridge APIs (file dialog, real filesystem) return stubs
known_limitations.impact: Workspace folder selection in onboarding falls back to a stub picker; the rest of the UI still works

known_limitations.issue: Agent silently falls back to mock responses when the FastAPI sidecar is unreachable
known_limitations.impact: Tests must explicitly assert the response is NOT the mock string to verify real streaming

known_limitations.issue: Backend's OpenAIProvider is hard-wired to base_url https://api.openai.com/v1 with a static catalogue
known_limitations.impact:
A real Groq run requires the OpenAIProvider's base_url to be overridable per-session. The Settings
Discover-Models flow still works (it accepts a base_url in the request body), but the actual
streamed agent run via /v1/sessions/{id}/agent/run will return
"model <groq-id> not found on provider openai" unless this is fixed.
